confj.name: ConfJ
confj.brief: Controls the configuration during joint movement
confj.usage: ConfJ (Configuration Joint) is used to specify whether or not the robot's configuration is to be controlled during joint movement. If it is not controlled, the robot can sometimes use a different configuration than that which was programmed. With ConfJ \Off, the robot cannot switch main axis configuration - it will search for a solution with the same main axis configuration as the current one, but it moves to the closest wrist configuration for axes 4 and 6. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in Motion tasks.

confl.name: ConfL
confl.brief: Monitors the configuration during linear movement
confl.usage: ConfL (Configuration Linear) is used to specify whether or not the robot's configuration is to be monitored during linear or circular movement. If it is not monitored, the configuration at execution time may differ from that at programmed time. It may also result in unexpected sweeping robot movements when the mode is changed to joint movement. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in Motion tasks. Note

connect.name: CONNECT
connect.brief: Connects an interrupt to a trap routine
connect.usage: CONNECT is used to find the identity of an interrupt and connect it to a trap routine. The interrupt is defined by ordering an interrupt event and specifying its identity. Thus, when that event occurs, the trap routine is automatically executed.

contactl.name: ContactL
contactl.usage: ContactL (Contact Linear) is used for the IRB 14000 robot to obtain contact with Description an object at a desired position while moving the tool center point (TCP) linearly. The collision detection level is raised to its maximum value, and during the movement the robot supervises the internal torque and compares it to a torque level given by the user. When the requested user torque level is reached, the robot performs a stiff stop and continues with the rest of the program. This instruction can typically be used when the tool held by the robot has to press an object into place.
contactl.syntax:
ContactL
[ '\' DesiredTorque ',']
[ ToPoint ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget >
[ '\' ID ':=' < expression (IN) of identno >] ','
[ Speed ':=' ] < expression (IN) of speeddata >
[ '\' Zone ':=' < expression (IN) of zonedata > ] ','
[ Tool ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of tooldata >
[ '\' WObj ':=' < persistent (PERS) of wobjdata > ] ';'

egmgetid.name: EGMGetId
egmgetid.usage: EGMGetId is used to reserve an EGM identity (EGMid). That identity is then used in all other EGM RAPID instructions and functions to identify a certain EGM process connected to the RAPID motion task from which it is used. An egmident is identified by its name, that is, a second or third call of EGMGetId with the same egmident will neither reserve a new EGM process nor change its content. To release an egmident for use by other EGM processes, the RAPID instruction EGMReset has to be used.

egmsetupao.usage: EGMSetupAO is used to set up AO signals for a specific EGM process (EGMid) as the source for position destination values to which the robot, and up to 6 additional axis, is to be guided. EGM joint mode is the only EGM mode that supports 7-axis robots. For 7-axis robots, the first additional axis input provides the position for the additional robot axis.
egmsetupao.syntax:
EGMSetupAO
[MecUnit ':='] <variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[EGMid ':='] <variable (VAR) of egmident> ','
[ExtConfigName ':='] <expression (IN) of string>
[['\'Joint] | ['\'Pose] | ['\'PathCorr]]
[['\'APTR] | ['\'LATR]]
['\'aoR1x ':=' <variable (VAR) of signalao>]
['\'aoR2y ':='] <variable (VAR) of signalao>]
['\'aoR3z ':='] <variable (VAR) of signalao>]
['\'aoR4rx ':='] <variable (VAR) of signalao>]
['\'aoR5ry ':='] <variable (VAR) of signalao>]
['\'aoR6rz ':='] <variable (VAR) of signalao>]
['\'

egmsetupgi.usage: EGMSetupGI is used to set up group input signals for a specific EGM process (EGMid) as the source for position destination values to which the robot, and up to 6 additional axis, is to be guided. EGM joint mode is the only EGM mode that supports 7-axis robots. For 7-axis robots, the first additional axis input provides the position for the additional robot axis.
egmsetupgi.syntax:
EGMSetupGI
[MecUnit ':='] <variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[EGMid ':='] <variable (VAR) of egmident> ','
[ExtConfigName ':='] <expression (IN) of string>
[['\'Joint] | ['\'Pose] | ['\'PathCorr]]
[['\'APTR] | ['\'LATR]]
['\'giR1x ':=' <variable (VAR) of signalgi>]
['\'giR2y ':=' <variable (VAR) of signalgi>]
['\'giR3z ':=' <variable (VAR) of signalgi>]
['\'giR4rx ':=' <variable (VAR) of signalgi>]
['\'giR5ry ':=' <variable (VAR) of signalgi>]
['\'giR6rz ':=' <variable (VAR) of signalgi>]
['\'giE1

egmsetupltapp.name: EGMSetupLTAPP
egmsetupltapp.brief: Setup the LTAPP protocol for EGM
egmsetupltapp.usage: EGMSetupLTAPP is used to set up an LTAPP protocol for a specific EGM process (EGMid) as the source for path corrections.
egmsetupltapp.syntax:
EGMSetupLTAPP
[MecUnit ':='] <variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[EGMid ':='] <variable (VAR) of egmident> ','
[ExtConfigName ':='] < expression (IN) of string> ','
[Device ':='] < expression (IN) of string> ','
[JointType ':='] < expression (IN) of num>
[['\'APTR] | ['\'LATR]] ';'

egmsetupuc.name: EGMSetupUC
egmsetupuc.brief: Setup the UdpUc protocol for EGM
egmsetupuc.usage: EGMSetupUC is used to set up a UdpUc protocol for a specific EGM process (EGMid) as the source for position destination values to which the robot, and up to 6 additional axis, is to be guided. EGM joint mode is the only EGM mode that supports 7-axis robots. For 7-axis robots, the first additional axis input provides the position for the additional robot axis.
egmsetupuc.syntax:
EGMSetupUC
[MecUnit ':='] <variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[EGMid ':='] <variable (VAR) of egmident> ','
[ExtConfigName ':='] <expression (IN) of string> ','
[UCDevice ':='] <expression (IN) of string>
[['\'Joint] | ['\'Pose] | ['\'PathCorr]]
[['\'APTR] | ['\'LATR]]
['\'CommTimeout ':=' <expression (IN) of num>] ';'

egmstop.name: EGMStop
egmstop.usage: EGMStop stops a specific EGM process (EGMid).
egmstop.syntax:
EGMStop
[EGMid ':='] <variable (VAR) of egmident>','
[Mode ':='] < expression (IN) of egmstopmode>
['\'RampOutTime ':=' <expression (IN) of num>] ';'

eoffsoff.name: EOffsOff
eoffsoff.brief: Deactivates an offset for additional axes
eoffsoff.usage: EOffsOff (External Offset Off) is used to deactivate an offset for additional axes. The offset for additional axes is activated by the instruction EOffsSet or EOffsOn and applies to all movements until some other offset for additional axes is activated or until the offset for additional axes is deactivated. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in Motion tasks.

eoffson.name: EOffsOn
eoffson.brief: Activates an offset for additional axes
eoffson.usage: EOffsOn (External Offset On) is used to define and activate an offset for additional axes using two positions. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in Motion tasks.
eoffson.syntax:
EOffsOn
[ '\' ExeP ':=' < expression (IN) of robtarget> ',']
[ ProgPoint ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget> ';'

eoffsset.name: EOffsSet
eoffsset.brief: Activates an offset for additional axes using known values
eoffsset.usage: EOffsSet (External Offset Set) is used to define and activate an offset for additional axes using known values.

erasemodule.name: EraseModule
erasemodule.usage: EraseModule is used to remove a module from the program memory during execution. There are no restrictions on how the module was loaded. It could have been loaded manually, from the configuration, or with a combination of the instructions Load, StartLoad, and WaitLoad. The module cannot be defined as Shared in the configuration.

errlog.name: ErrLog
errlog.usage: ErrLog is used to display an error message on the FlexPendant and write it in the event log. Error number and five error arguments must be stated. The message
errlog.syntax:
ErrLog
[ErrorId ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ','
[ '\'W ] | [' \' I ] ','
[Argument1 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ','
[Argument2 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ','
[Argument3 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ','
[Argument4 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ','
[Argument5 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ';'

errraise.name: ErrRaise
errraise.brief: Writes a warning and calls an error handler
errraise.usage: ErrRaise is used to create an error in the program and then call the error handler of the routine. A warning is written in the event log. ErrRaise can also be used
errraise.syntax:
ErrRaise
[ErrorName ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string> ','
[ErrorId ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ','
[Argument1 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ','
[Argument2 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ','
[Argument3 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ','
[Argument4 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ','
[Argument5 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errstr> ';'

errwrite.name: ErrWrite
errwrite.usage: ErrWrite (Error Write) is used to display an error message on the FlexPendant
errwrite.syntax:
ErrWrite
[ '\'W ] | [ '\' I ] ','
[ Header ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string>','
[ Reason ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string>
[ '\'RL2 ':=' < expression (IN) of string> ]
[ '\'RL3 ':=' < expression (IN) of string> ]
[ '\'RL4 ':=' < expression (IN) of string> ] ';'
examples: ErrWrite "PLC error", "Fatal error in PLC" \RL2:="Call service";
ErrWrite \W, "Search error", "No hit for the first search";

exit.name: EXIT
exit.usage: EXIT is used to terminate program execution. Program restart will then be blocked, that is the program can only be restarted from the first instruction of the main routine. The EXIT instruction should be used when fatal errors occur or when program execution is to be stopped permanently. The Stop instruction is used to temporarily stop program execution. After execution of the instruction EXIT the program pointer is gone. To continue program execution, the program pointer must be set.

exitcycle.name: ExitCycle
exitcycle.brief: Break current cycle and start next
exitcycle.usage: ExitCycle is used to break the current cycle and move the program pointer (PP) back to the first instruction in the main routine. If the program is executed in continuous mode, it will start to execute the next cycle. If the execution is in cycle mode, the execution will stop at the first instruction in the main routine.

fitcircle.name: FitCircle
fitcircle.usage: FitCircle is used to fit a circle to a set of 3D-points.
fitcircle.syntax:
CircleFit
[ Points ':='] <array {*} expression (IN) of dnum> ','
[ '\' NumPoints ':=' < expression (IN) of num>] ','
[ Center ':='] <variable (VAR) of pos> ','
[ Radius ':='] <variable (VAR) of num> ','
[ Normal ':='] <variable (VAR) of pos>
[ '\' RMS ':=' <variable (VAR) of num>]
[ '\' MaxErr ':=' <variable (VAR) of num> ]
[ '\' IndexWorst ':=' <variable (VAR) of num> ] ';'

for.name: FOR
for.brief: Repeats a given number of times
for.usage: FOR is used when one or several instructions are to be repeated a number of times.
for.syntax:
FOR <loop variable> FROM <expression> TO <expression>
[ STEP <expression> ] DO
<statement list>
ENDFOR

fricidinit.name: FricIdInit
fricidinit.usage: FricIdInit marks the starting point of a sequence of move instructions that will be repeated to calculate the robots internal friction.

fricidevaluate.name: FricIdEvaluate
fricidevaluate.usage: FricIdEvaluate makes the robot repeat the movement between the instructions FricIdInit and FricIdEvaluate while calculating the friction for each axis of the robot.
fricidevaluate.syntax:
FricIdEvaluate
[ FricLevels ':='] < persistent array {*} (PERS) of num >
['\' MechUnit ':=' < variable (VAR) of mecunit >]
['\' BwdSpeed ':=' < expression (IN) of speeddata >]
['\' NoPrint]
['\' FricLevelMax ':=' < expression (VAR) of num >]
['\' FricLevelMin ':=' < expression (VAR) of num >]
['\' OptTolerance ':=' < expression (VAR) of num >] ';'

fricidsetfriclevels.name: FricIdSetFricLevels
fricidsetfriclevels.brief: Set friction levels after friction identification
fricidsetfriclevels.usage: FricIdSetFricLevels is used for setting the friction level for each axis of a mechanical unit.
fricidsetfriclevels.syntax:
FricIdSetFricLevels
[ FricLevels ':='] < array {*} (IN) of num >
['\' MechUnit ':=' < variable (VAR) of mecunit >] ';'

getdataval.name: GetDataVal
getdataval.brief: Get the value of a data object
getdataval.usage: GetDataVal (Get Data Value) makes it possible to get a value from a data object
getdataval.syntax:
GetDataVal
[ Object ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string >
['\'Block' :='<variable (VAR) of datapos>]
|[ '\'TaskRef' :=' <variable (VAR) of taskid>]
|[ '\'TaskName' :=' <expression (IN) of string>] ',']
[ Value ':=' ] <variable (VAR) of anytype>]';'

getjointdata.name: GetJointData
getjointdata.usage: GetJointData is used to read out joint specific data from a specified mechanical unit. The information that can be read for the specified axis is the position, speed, torque, and estimated external torque.
getjointdata.syntax:
GetJointData
['\' MechUnit ':=' < variable (VAR) of mecunit> ',']
[Axis ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\' Position ':=' < variable (VAR) of num> ]
[ '\' Speed ':=' < variable (VAR) of num> ]
[ '\' Torque ':=' < variable (VAR) of num> ]
[ '\' ExtTorque ':=' < variable (VAR) of num> ] ';'

getsysdata.name: GetSysData
getsysdata.usage: GetSysData fetches the value and the optional symbol name for the current system data of specified data type. With this instruction it is possible to fetch data and the name of the current active Tool, Work Object, PayLoad or Total Load for the robot in actual or connected motion task, or any named motion task.
getsysdata.syntax:
GetSysData
['\' TaskRef' :=' <variable (VAR) of taskid>]
|['\' TaskName' :=' <expression (IN) of string>]
[ DestObject' :='] < persistent(PERS) of anytype>
['\'ObjectName' :=' < variable or persistent (INOUT) of string>
] ';'

gettrapdata.name: GetTrapData
gettrapdata.brief: Get interrupt data for current TRAP
gettrapdata.usage: GetTrapData is used in a trap routine to obtain all information about the interrupt that caused the trap routine to be executed.

goto.name: GOTO
goto.usage: GOTO is used to transfer program execution to another line (a label) within the same

gripload.name: GripLoad
gripload.brief: Defines the payload for a robot
gripload.usage: GripLoad is used to define the payload which the robot holds in its gripper. Description GripLoad specifies which load the robot is carrying. Specified load is used by the control system so that the robot movements can be controlled in the best possible way. The payload is connected/disconnected using the instruction GripLoad, which adds or subtracts the weight of the payload to the weight of the gripper. WARNING

hollowwristreset.name: HollowWristReset
hollowwristreset.usage: HollowWristReset (Reset hollow wrist) resets the position of the wrist joints on hollow wrist manipulators, such as IRB5402 and IRB5403. The instruction makes it possible to avoid rewinding the wrist joints 4 and 5 after they have been wound up one or more revolutions. After executing a HollowWristReset instruction, the wrist joints may continue to wind up in the same direction. Description HollowWristReset makes it easier to make application programs. You do not have to ensure that the wrist position is within �2 revolutions at the time of

icap.name: ICap
icap.brief: connect CAP events to trap routines
icap.usage: ICap is used to connect an interrupt number (which is already connected to a trap
icap.syntax:
ICap
[Interrupt ':='] < variable (IN) of intnum > ','
[Event ':='] < variable (IN) of num > ';'

idelete.name: IDelete
idelete.usage: IDelete (Interrupt Delete) is used to cancel (delete) an interrupt subscription. If the interrupt is to be only temporarily disabled, the instruction ISleep or IDisable should be used.

idisable.name: IDisable
idisable.usage: IDisable(Interrupt Disable) is used to disable all interrupts temporarily. It may, for example, be used in a particularly sensitive part of the program where no interrupts may be permitted to take place if they disturb normal program execution.

ienable.name: IEnable
ienable.usage: IEnable(Interrupt Enable) is used to enable interrupts during program execution.

ierror.name: IError
ierror.usage: IError (Interrupt Errors) is used to order and enable an interrupt when an error
ierror.syntax:
IError
[ErrorDomain ':='] <expression (IN) of errdomain>
['\'ErrorId':=' <expression (IN) of num>\\ ','
[ErrorType' :='] <expression (IN) of errtype> ','
[Interrupt' :='] <variable (VAR) of intnum>';'

if.name: IF
if.brief: If a condition is met, then
if.usage: IF is used when different instructions are to be executed depending on whether a condition is met or not.
if.syntax:
IF <conditional expression> THEN
<statement list>
{ ELSEIF <conditional expression> THEN
<statement list> | <EIT> }
[ ELSE
<statement list> ]
ENDIF

incr.name: Incr
incr.usage: Incr is used to add 1 to a numeric variable or persistent.
incr.syntax:
Incr
[ Name ':=' ] < var or pers (INOUT) of num >
| [ Dname' :=' ] < var or pers (INOUT) of dnum >' ;'

indamove.name: IndAMove
indamove.usage: IndAMove(Independent Absolute Movement) is used to change an axis to independent mode and move the axis to a specific position. An independent axis is an axis moving independently of other axes in the robot system. As program execution immediately continues, it is possible to execute other instructions (including positioning instructions) during the time the independent axis is moving. If the axis is to be moved within a revolution, the instruction IndRMove should be used instead. If the move is to occur a short distance from the current position,
indamove.syntax:
IndAMove
[ MecUnit':=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit>' ,'
[ Axis':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\'ToAbsPos':=' < expression (IN) of robtarget> ]
| [ '\' ToAbsNum':=' < expression (IN) of num> ] ','
[ Speed ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\' Ramp':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'
Independent Axis

indcmove.name: IndCMove
indcmove.usage: IndCMove (Independent Continuous Movement) is used to change an axis to independent mode and start the axis moving continuously at a specific speed. An independent axis is an axis moving independently of other axes in the robot system. As program execution continues immediately it is possible to execute other instructions (including positioning instructions) during the time the independent axis is moving. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in Motion tasks.
indcmove.syntax:
IndCMove
[ MecUnit':=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit>' ,'
[ Axis':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ','
[ Speed ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\' Ramp':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

inddmove.name: IndDMove
inddmove.usage: IndDMove(Independent Delta Movement) is used to change an axis to independent mode and move the axis to a specific distance.
inddmove.syntax:
IndDMove
[ MecUnit':=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[ Axis':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ','
[ Delta':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>','
[ Speed '=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\' Ramp':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

indreset.name: IndReset
indreset.usage: IndReset (Independent Reset) is used to change an independent axis back to
indreset.syntax:
IndReset
[ MecUnit':=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[ Axis ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\' RefPos ':=' < expression (IN) of robtarget> ] |
[ '\' RefNum ':=' < expression (IN) of num> ]
[ '\' Short ] | [ '\' Fwd ] | [ '\' Bwd ] | [ '\' Old ]';'

indrmove.name: IndRMove
indrmove.usage: IndRMove (Independent Relative Movement) is used to change a rotational axis to independent mode and move the axis to a specific position within one revolution. An independent axis is an axis moving independently of other axes in the robot system. As program execution continues immediately it is possible to execute other instructions (including positioning instructions) during the time the independent axis is moving. If the axis is to be moved to an absolute position (several revolutions) or if the axis is linear, the instruction IndAMove is used instead. If the movement is to take place
indrmove.syntax:
IndRMove
[ MecUnit ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[ Axis ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\' ToRelPos ':=' < expression (IN) of robtargets> ]
| [ '\' ToRelNum ':=' < expression (IN) of num> ]
[ '\' Short ] | [ '\' Fwd ] | [ '\' Bwd ] ','
[ Speed ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\' Ramp ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

initsuperv.name: InitSuperv
initsuperv.usage: InitSuperv is used to initiate CAP supervision. This means that all supervision lists will be cleared and all I/O subscriptions will be removed.

invertdo.name: InvertDO
invertdo.brief: Inverts the value of a digital output signal
invertdo.usage: InvertDO (Invert Digital Output) inverts the value of a digital output signal (0 -> 1 and 1 -> 0).

iobusstart.name: IOBusStart
iobusstart.usage: IOBusStart is used to start a certain I/O network.

iobusstate.name: IOBusState
iobusstate.brief: Get current state of I/O network
iobusstate.usage: IOBusState is used to read the state of a certain I/O network. Its physical state
iobusstate.syntax:
IOBusState
[ BusName ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string> ','
[ State ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of busstate>
[ '\' Phys] | [ '\' Logic] ';'

iodisable.name: IODisable
iodisable.usage: IODisable is used to deactivate an I/O device during program execution. I/O devices are automatically activated after start-up if they are defined in the system parameters. When required for some reason, I/O devices can be deactivated or activated during program execution. Note It is not possible to deactivate an I/O device with Unit Trustlevel set to Required.
iodisable.syntax:
IODisable
[ UnitName ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string> ','
[ MaxTime ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ';'

ioenable.name: IOEnable
ioenable.usage: IOEnable is used to activate an I/O device during program execution. I/O devices are automatically activated after start-up if they are defined in the system parameters. When required for some reason I/O devices can be deactivated or activated during program execution. The controller action when activating an I/O device depends on the defined Unit Trustlevel in the system parameters.
ioenable.syntax:
IOEnable
[ UnitName ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string>' ,'
[ MaxTime' :=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ';'

ipathpos.name: IPathPos
ipathpos.brief: Get center line robtarget when weaving
ipathpos.usage: IPathPos is used to retrieve the position of the center line during weaving with CAP. This function is mainly used together with the tracking functionality. It is necessary to activate weaving and the synchronization signals on both the left side and the right side.
ipathpos.syntax:
IPathPos
[p_robt ':='] < persistent (PERS) of robtarget > ','
Continuous Application Platform (CAP)
[sen_pos ':='] < persistent (PERS) of pos > ','
[Interrupt ':='] < variable (IN) of intnum >
['\' EOffs ]
['\' NoDispl ] ';'

ipers.name: IPers
ipers.brief: Interrupt at value change of a persistent variable
ipers.usage: IPers (Interrupt Persistent) is used to order and enable interrupts to be generated when the value of a persistent variable is changed.
ipers.syntax:
IPers
[ '\' Single ] | [ '\' SingleSafe ] ','
[ Name ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of anytype > ','
[ Interrupt' :=' ] < variable (VAR) of intnum > ';'

irmqmessage.name: IRMQMessage
irmqmessage.brief: Orders RMQ interrupts for a data type
irmqmessage.usage: IRMQMessage (Interrupt RAPID Message Queue Message) is used to order and enable interrupts for a specific data type when using RMQ function.
irmqmessage.syntax:
IRMQMessage
[ InterruptDataType ':=' ] < reference (REF) of anytype >
[ Interrupt ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of intnum >';'

isignalai.name: ISignalAI
isignalai.usage: ISignalAI (Interrupt Signal Analog Input) is used to order and enable interrupts from an analog input signal.
isignalai.syntax:
ISignalAI
[ '\' Single ] | [ '\' SingleSafe ] ','
[ Signal ':=' ] <variable (VAR) of signalai> ','
[ Condition ':=' ] <expression (IN) of aiotrigg> ','
[ HighValue ':=' ] <expression (IN) of num> ','
[ LowValue ':=' ] <expression (IN) of num> ','
[ DeltaValue ':=' ] <expression (IN) of num>
[['\'DPos] | [ '\' DNeg] ',']
[ Interrupt ':=' ] <variable (VAR) of intnum> ';'

isignalao.name: ISignalAO
isignalao.usage: ISignalAO (Interrupt Signal Analog Output) is used to order and enable interrupts from an analog output signal.
isignalao.syntax:
ISignalAO
[ '\' Single ] | [ '\' SingleSafe ] ','
[ Signal':=' ]<variable (VAR) of signalao>','
[ Condition':=' ]<expression (IN) of aiotrigg>','
[ HighValue':=' ]<expression (IN) of num>','
[ LowValue':=' ]<expression (IN) of num>','
[ DeltaValue':=' ]<expression (IN) of num>
[['\'DPos] | [ '\'DNeg] ',']
[ Interrupt':=' ]<variable (VAR) of intnum>';'

isignaldi.name: ISignalDI
isignaldi.brief: Orders interrupts from a digital input signal
isignaldi.usage: ISignalDI (Interrupt Signal Digital In) is used to order and enable interrupts from a digital input signal.
isignaldi.syntax:
ISignalDI
[ '\' Single ] | [ '\' SingleSafe ] ','
[ Signal ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of signaldi > ','
[ TriggValue' :=' ] < expression (IN) of dionum > ','
[ Interrupt' :=' ] < variable (VAR) of intnum > ';'

isignaldo.name: ISignalDO
isignaldo.brief: Interrupts from a digital output signal
isignaldo.usage: ISignalDO (Interrupt Signal Digital Out) is used to order and enable interrupts from a digital output signal.
isignaldo.syntax:
ISignalDO
[ '\' Single ] | [ '\' SingleSafe ] ','
[ Signal ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of signaldo > ','
[ TriggValue' :=' ] < expression (IN) of dionum > ','
[ Interrupt' :=' ] < variable (VAR) of intnum > ';'

isignalgi.name: ISignalGI
isignalgi.brief: Orders interrupts from a group of digital input signals
isignalgi.usage: ISignalGI (Interrupt Signal Group Digital In) is used to order and enable interrupts from a group of digital input signals.
isignalgi.syntax:
ISignalGI
[ '\' Single ] | [ '\' SingleSafe ] ','
[ Signal ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of signalgi > ','
[ Interrupt':=' ] < variable (VAR) of intnum > ';'

isignalgo.name: ISignalGO
isignalgo.brief: Orders interrupts from a group of digital output signals
isignalgo.usage: ISignalGO (Interrupt Signal Group Digital Out) is used to order and enable interrupts from a group of digital output signals.
isignalgo.syntax:
ISignalGO
[ '\' Single ] | [ '\' SingleSafe ] ','
[ Signal ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of signalgo > ','
[ Interrupt':=' ] < variable (VAR) of intnum > ';'

isleep.name: ISleep
isleep.usage: ISleep(Interrupt Sleep) is used to deactivate an individual interrupt temporarily. During the deactivation time any generated interrupts of the specified type are discarded without any trap execution.

itimer.name: ITimer
itimer.usage: ITimer (Interrupt Timer) is used to order and enable a timed interrupt. This instruction can be used, for example, to check the status of peripheral equipment once every minute.
itimer.syntax:
ITimer
[ '\' Single ] | [ '\' SingleSafe ] ','
[ Time ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num >','
[ Interrupt' :=' ] < variable (VAR) of intnum > ';'

ivarvalue.name: IVarValue
ivarvalue.usage: IVarValue (Interrupt Variable Value) is used to order and enable an interrupt when the value of a variable accessed via the serial sensor interface has been changed. This instruction can be used, for example, to get seam volume or gap values from a seam tracker.
ivarvalue.syntax:
IVarValue
[ device ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string> ','
[ VarNo ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ Value ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of num > ','
[ Interrupt ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of intnum > ','
[ '\' Unit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ '\' DeadBand ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ '\' ReportAtTool ] ','
[ '\' SpeedAdapt ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ '\' APTR ] ';'

iwatch.name: IWatch
iwatch.usage: IWatch(Interrupt Watch) is used to activate an interrupt which was previously ordered but was deactivated with ISleep.

label.name: Label

pathacclim.brief: Reduce TCP acceleration along the path
pathacclim.usage: PathAccLim (Path Acceleration Limitation) is used to set or reset limitations on TCP acceleration and/or TCP deceleration along the movement path. The limitation will be performed along the movement path, that is, the acceleration in the path frame. It is the tangential acceleration/deceleration in the path direction that will be limited. The instruction does not limit the total acceleration of the equipment, that is, the acceleration in world frame, so it cannot be directly used to protect the equipment from large accelerations.
pathacclim.syntax:
PathAccLim
[ AccLim ':=' ] < expression (IN) of bool >
[ '\' AccMax ':=' <expression (IN) of num >] ','
[ DecelLim ':=' ] < expression (IN) of bool>
[ '\' DecelMax ':=' <expression (IN) of num >] ';'

pathrecmovebwd.name: PathRecMoveBwd
pathrecmovebwd.usage: PathRecMoveBwd is used to move the robot backwards along a recorded path.
pathrecmovebwd.syntax:
PathRecMoveBwd
[ '\' ID ':=' < variable (VAR) of pathrecid > ]
[ '\' ToolOffs ':=' <expression (IN) of pos> ]
[ '\' Speed ':=' <expression (IN) of speeddata> ]';'

pathrecmovefwd.name: PathRecMoveFwd
pathrecmovefwd.usage: PathRecMoveFwd is used to move the robot back to the position where
pathrecmovefwd.syntax:
PathRecMoveFwd '('
[ '\' ID ':=' < variable (VAR) of pathid > ]
[ '\' ToolOffs ':=' <expression (IN) of pos> ]
[ '\' Speed ':=' <expression (IN) of speeddata> ]';'

pathrecstart.name: PathRecStart
pathrecstart.usage: PathRecStart is used to start recording the robot's path. The path recorder will store path information during execution of the RAPID program.

pathrecstop.name: PathRecStop
pathrecstop.usage: PathRecStop is used to stop recording the robot's path.

pathresol.name: PathResol
pathresol.usage: PathResol (Path Resolution) is used to override the configured geometric path sample time defined in the system parameters for the mechanical units that are controlled from current program task. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in any motion tasks. Description The path resolution affects the accuracy of the interpolated path and the program cycle time. The path accuracy is improved and the cycle time is often reduced

pdispoff.name: PDispOff
pdispoff.usage: PDispOff (Program Displacement Off) is used to deactivate a program displacement. Program displacement is activated by the instruction PDispSet or PDispOn and applies to all movements until some other program displacement is activated or until program displacement is deactivated. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in Motion tasks.

pdispon.name: PDispOn
pdispon.usage: PDispOn (Program Displacement On) is used to define and activate a program displacement using two robot positions.
pdispon.syntax:
PDispOn
[ [ '\' Rot ]
['\' ExeP ':=' < expression (IN) of robtarget>]',']
[ ProgPoint' :=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget> ','
[ Tool ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of tooldata>
[ '\'WObj' :=' < persistent (PERS) of wobjdata> ] ';'

pdispset.name: PDispSet
pdispset.brief: Activates program displacement using known frame
pdispset.usage: PDispSet (Program Displacement Set) is used to define and activate a program displacement using known frame.

proccall.name: ProcCall
proccall.usage: A procedure call is used to transfer program execution to another procedure. When the procedure has been fully executed the program execution continues with the instruction following the procedure call. It is usually possible to send a number of arguments to the new procedure. These control the behavior of the procedure and make it possible for the same procedure to be used for different things.

procerrrecovery.name: ProcerrRecovery
procerrrecovery.brief: Generate and recover from process-move error
procerrrecovery.usage: ProcerrRecovery can be used to generate process error during robot movement and get the possibility to handle the error and restart the process and the movement from an ERROR handler.
procerrrecovery.syntax:
ProcerrRecovery
[ '\' SyncOrgMoveInst ] | [' \' SyncLastMoveInst ]
[ '\' ProcSignal' :=' ] < variable (VAR) of signaldo > ';'

prxactivandstorerecord.name: PrxActivAndStoreRecord
prxactivandstorerecord.brief: Activate and store the recorded profile data
prxactivandstorerecord.usage: PrxActivAndStoreRecord is used to activate the recorded profile data and store it in a file. Can be used instead of calling both PrxActivRecord and PrxStoreRecord.
prxactivandstorerecord.syntax:
PrxActivAndStoreRecord
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of mechunit> ','
[ Delay ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
Machine Synchronization
[ File_name ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ';'

prxactivrecord.name: PrxActivRecord
prxactivrecord.usage: PrxActivRecord is used to activate the record that was just recorded in order to use it without having to save it before.
prxactivrecord.syntax:
PrxActivRecord
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of mechunit> ','
[ Delay ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ';'
Machine Synchronization

prxdbgstorerecord.name: PrxDbgStoreRecord
prxdbgstorerecord.brief: Store and debug the recorded profile data
prxdbgstorerecord.usage: PrxDbgStoreRecord is used to store a non activated record for debug. Can be used to compare recordings and check the repeatability.
prxdbgstorerecord.syntax:
PrxDbgStoreRecord
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of mechunit> ','
[ File_name ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ';'

prxdeactrecord.name: PrxDeactRecord
prxdeactrecord.usage: PrxDeactRecord is used to deactivate a record.

prxresetpos.name: PrxResetPos
prxresetpos.brief: Reset the zero position of the sensor
prxresetpos.usage: PrxResetPos is used to reset the zero position of the sensor. The sensor position is reset for synchronization functionality and recorded file but the I/O signal value is not reset. This instruction is used for software reset of sensor input where no sync switch is available to reset the I/O signal.

prxresetrecords.name: PrxResetRecords
prxresetrecords.usage: PrxResetRecords is used to reset and deactivate all records.

prxsetposoffset.name: PrxSetPosOffset
prxsetposoffset.brief: Set a reference position for the sensor
prxsetposoffset.usage: PrxSetPosOffset is used to set a reference position for the sensor. The sensor position is set to reference for synchronization functionality and recorded file. This function is used for software set of sensor reference where no sync switch is available to reset the I/O signal.
prxsetposoffset.syntax:
PrxSetPosOffset
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of mechunit> ','
[ Reference ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ';'

prxsetrecordsampletime.name: PrxSetRecordSampleTime
prxsetrecordsampletime.brief: Set the sample time for recording a profile
prxsetrecordsampletime.usage: PrxSetRecordSampleTime is used to set the sample time, in seconds, for recording a profile. The default sample time is taken from the system parameter Pos Update time, belonging to type CAN interface in the topic Process. Note that Pos Update time specifies the sample time in milliseconds, while PrxSetRecordSampleTime specifies the sample time in seconds. The maximum number of samples in a recorded profile is 300. If a recording is longer than 300 * Pos Update time, the sample time must be increased.
prxsetrecordsampletime.syntax:
PrxSetRecordSampleTime
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of mechunit> ','
[ SampleTime ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ';'

prxsetsyncalarm.name: PrxSetSyncalarm
prxsetsyncalarm.usage: PrxSetSyncalarm is used to set sync_alarm_signal behavior to a pulse during specified time. If sync alarm is triggered, the Sync_alarm_signal is pulsed during the time specified by the instruction PrxSetSyncalarm. It can also be set to no pulse, that is, the signal continues to be high. The default pulse length is 1 sec.
prxsetsyncalarm.syntax:
PrxSetSyncalarm
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of mechunit>
[ '\' Time ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ]
| [ '\' NoPulse ] ';'

prxstartrecord.name: PrxStartRecord
prxstartrecord.usage: PrxStartRecord is used to reset all profile data and record a new profile of the sensor movement as soon as sensor_start_signal is set. To be able to make a recording it is important to first make a connection to a sensor (mechanical unit whose speed affects the speed of the robot). This means that a WaitSensor instruction has to be executed before the recording starts.
prxstartrecord.syntax:
PrxStartRecord
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of mechunit> ','
[ Record_duration ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ Profile_type ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ';'

prxstoprecord.name: PrxStopRecord
prxstoprecord.usage: PrxStopRecord is used to stop recording a profile. Should always be used when PrxStartRecord has Record_duration set to 0.

prxstorerecord.name: PrxStoreRecord
prxstorerecord.usage: PrxStoreRecord is used to save an activated record in a file.

prxusefilerecord.name: PrxUseFileRecord
prxusefilerecord.usage: PrxUseFileRecord is used to load and activate a record from a file for sensor synchronization.
prxusefilerecord.syntax:
PrxUseFileRecord
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < expression (IN) of mechunit> ','
[ Delay ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ File_name ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ';'

pulsedo.name: PulseDO
pulsedo.brief: Generates a pulse on a digital output signal
pulsedo.usage: PulseDO is used to generate a pulse on a digital output signal.
pulsedo.syntax:
PulseDO
[ '\' High]
[ '\' PLength ':=' < expression (IN) of num >] ','
[ Signal ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of signaldo > ';'

raise.name: RAISE
raise.usage: RAISE is used to create an error in the program and then to call the error handler of the routine.

raisetouser.name: RaiseToUser
raisetouser.brief: Propagates an error to user level
raisetouser.usage: RaiseToUseris used in an error handler in nostepin routines to propagate the current error or any other defined error to the error handler at user level. User level is in this case the first routine in a call chain above a nostepin routine.
raisetouser.syntax:
RaiseToUser
[ '\' Continue ]
'|' [ '\' BreakOff ]
[ '\' ErrorNumber ':=' ] < expression (IN) of errnum> ';'

readanybin.name: ReadAnyBin

readanybin.usage: ReadAnyBin (Read Any Binary) is used to read any type of data from a binary serial channel or file.

readblock.brief: read a block of data from device
readblock.usage: ReadBlock is used to read a block of data from a device connected to the serial sensor interface. The data is stored in a file. The sensor interface communicates with two sensors over serial channels using the RTP1 transport protocol. This is an example of a sensor channel configuration. COM_PHY_CHANNEL: � Name "COM1:" � Connector "COM1"

readrawbytes.usage: ReadRawBytes is used to read data of type rawbytes from a device opened with
readrawbytes.syntax:

readvararr.name: ReadVarArr
readvararr.brief: Read multiple variables from a sensor device
readvararr.usage: ReadVarArr is used to read up to six variables at the same time from a sensor device. The result is from the same sample. The sensor must be configured and communicating via the RobotWare option Sensor Interface.

removefile.usage: RemoveFile is used to remove a file. The user must have write and execute permission for the directory where the file resides and the user must have write permission for the file itself.

reset.usage: Reset is used to reset the value of a digital output signal to zero.

resetppmoved.name: ResetPPMoved
resetppmoved.brief: Reset state for the program pointer moved in manual mode
resetppmoved.usage: ResetPPMoved reset state for the program pointer moved in manual mode. PPMovedInManMode returns TRUE if the user has moved the program pointer while the controller is in manual mode - that is, the operator key is at Man Reduced Speed or Man Full Speed. The program pointer moved state is reset when the key is switched from Auto to Man, or when using the instruction ResetPPMoved.

resetretrycount.name: ResetRetryCount
resetretrycount.usage: ResetRetryCount is used to reset the number of retries that has been done from an error handler. The maximum number of retries that can be done is defined in the configuration.

restopath.name: RestoPath
restopath.brief: Restores the path after an interrupt
restopath.usage: RestoPath is used to restore a path that was stored at a previous stage using the instruction StorePath. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in Motion tasks.

retry.name: RETRY
retry.usage: The RETRY instruction is used to resume program execution after an error starting with (re-executing) the instruction that caused the error.

return.name: RETURN
return.usage: RETURN is used to finish the execution of a routine. If the routine is a function then

rewind.name: Rewind
rewind.usage: Rewind sets the file position to the beginning of the file.

rmqemptyqueue.name: RMQEmptyQueue
rmqemptyqueue.usage: RMQEmptyQueue empties the RAPID Message Queue (RMQ) in the task that is executing the instruction.

If using this instruction, the user needs to know what kind of data type will be sent in the answer from the client.

RMQSendWait

setdo.name: SetDO
setdo.brief: Changes the value of a digital output signal
setdo.usage: SetDO is used to change the value of a digital output signal, with or without a time
setdo.syntax:
SetDO
[ '\' SDelay ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ',' ]
|['\'Sync',']
[ Signal ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of signaldo > ','
[ Value ':=' ] < expression (IN) of dionum > ';'

setgo.name: SetGO
setgo.brief: Changes the value of a group of digital output signals
setgo.usage: SetGO is used to change the value of a group of digital output signals with or
setgo.syntax:
SetGO
[ '\' SDelay ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ',' ]
[ Signal ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of signalgo > ','
[ Value ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num >
| [ Dvalue' :=' ] < expression (IN) of dnum > ';'

setleadthrough.name: SetLeadThrough
setleadthrough.usage: SetLeadThrough is used to activate and deactivate lead-through for a TCP robot.

setsysdata.name: SetSysData
setsysdata.usage: SetSysData activates the specified system data name for the specified data type. With this instruction it is possible to change the current active Tool, Work Object, PayLoad or Total Load for the robot in actual or connected motion task.
setsysdata.syntax:
SetSysData
[ SourceObject':='] < persistent(PERS) of anytype>
['\'ObjectName':=' < expression (IN) of string> ] ';'

setupcyclicbool.name: SetupCyclicBool
setupcyclicbool.usage: SetupCyclicBool is used to set up a logical condition that cyclically will be evaluated and assigned to a persistent boolean variable, a Cyclic bool.
setupcyclicbool.syntax:
SetupCyclicBool
[ Flag ':=' ] <persistent (PERS) of bool> ','
[ Cond ':=' ] <expression (IN) of bool>
['\' Signal ':=' <variable (VAR) of signaldo>] ';'

setupsuperv.name: SetupSuperv
setupsuperv.brief: Setup conditions for signal supervision in CAP
setupsuperv.usage: SetupSuperv is used to set up conditions for I/O signals to be supervised. The conditions are collected in different lists:
setupsuperv.syntax:
SetupSuperv
[Signal ':='] < variable (VAR) of signaldi > ','
[Condition ':='] < variable (IN) of num > ','
[Listtype ':='] < variable (IN) of num >
[\ErrIndSig ':=' < variable (VAR) of signaldo >] ';'

siconnect.name: SiConnect
siconnect.usage: SiConnect is used to establish a connection to an external device.
siconnect.syntax:
SiConnect
[ Sensor ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of sensor >
[ '\' NoStop ] ';'

siclose.name: SiClose
siclose.usage: SiClose closes an existing connection to an external device.

sigetcyclic.name: SiGetCyclic
sigetcyclic.usage: SiGetCyclic subscribes data for cyclic transmission from an external device.
sigetcyclic.syntax:
SiGetCyclic
[ Sensor ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of sensor > ','
[ Data ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of anytype > ','
[ Rate ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

singarea.name: SingArea
singarea.usage: SingArea is used to define how the robot is to move in the proximity of singular points.

sisetcyclic.name: SiSetCyclic
sisetcyclic.usage: SiSetCyclic registers data for cyclic transmission to an external device.
sisetcyclic.syntax:
SiSetCyclic
[ Sensor ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of sensor > ','
[ Data ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of anytype >
[ Rate ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

skipwarn.name: SkipWarn
skipwarn.usage: SkipWarn(Skip Warning) is used to skip the latest generated warning message to be stored in the Event Log during execution in running mode continuously or cycle (no warnings skipped in FWD or BWD step). With SkipWarn it is possible to repeatedly do error recovery in RAPID without filling the Event Log with only warning messages.

socketaccept.name: SocketAccept
socketaccept.usage: SocketAccept is used to accept incoming connection requests. SocketAccept can only be used for server applications.
socketaccept.syntax:
SocketAccept
[ Socket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev > ','
[ ClientSocket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev >
[ '\' ClientAddress ':=' < variable (VAR) of string> ]
[ '\' Time ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

socketbind.name: SocketBind
socketbind.brief: Bind a socket to my IP-address and port
socketbind.usage: SocketBind is used to bind a socket to the specified server IP-address and port number. SocketBind can only be used for server applications.
socketbind.syntax:
SocketBind
[ Socket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev > ','
[ LocalAddress ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ','
[ LocalPort ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ';'

socketclose.name: SocketClose
socketclose.usage: SocketClose is used when a socket connection is no longer going to be used. After a socket has been closed it cannot be used in any socket call except SocketCreate.

socketconnect.name: SocketConnect
socketconnect.usage: SocketConnect is used to connect the socket to a remote computer in a client application.
socketconnect.syntax:
SocketConnect
[ Socket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev > ','
[ Address ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ','
[ Port ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num >
[ '\' Time ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

socketcreate.name: SocketCreate
socketcreate.usage: SocketCreate is used to create a new socket for connection based communication or connectionless communication. Both socket messaging of stream type protocol TCP/IP with delivery guarantee and datagram protocol UDP/IP is supported. Both server and client application can be developed. For datagram protocol UDP/IP, broadcast is supported.
socketcreate.syntax:
SocketCreate
[ Socket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev >
[ '\' UDP ] ';'

socketlisten.name: SocketListen
socketlisten.usage: SocketListen is used to start listening for incoming connections, i.e. start acting as a server. SocketListen can only used for server applications.

socketreceive.name: SocketReceive
socketreceive.usage: SocketReceive is used for receiving data from a remote computer. SocketReceive can be used both for client and server applications.
socketreceive.syntax:
SocketReceive
[ Socket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev >
[ '\' Str ':=' < variable (VAR) of string > ]
| [ '\' RawData ':=' < variable (VAR) of rawbytes > ]
| [ '\' Data ':=' < array {*} (VAR) of byte > ]
[ '\' ReadNoOfBytes ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ]
[ '\' NoRecBytes ':=' < variable (VAR) of num > ]
[ '\' Time ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

socketreceivefrom.name: SocketReceiveFrom
socketreceivefrom.usage: SocketReceiveFrom is used for receiving data from a remote computer.
socketreceivefrom.syntax:
SocketReceiveFrom
[ Socket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev >
[ '\' Str ':=' < variable (VAR) of string > ]
| [ '\' RawData ':=' < variable (VAR) of rawbytes > ]
| [ '\' Data ':=' < array {*} (VAR) of byte > ]
[ '\' NoRecBytes ':=' < variable (VAR) of num > ]
[ RemoteAddress ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of string >
[ RemotePort ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of num >
[ '\' Time ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

socketsend.name: SocketSend
socketsend.usage: SocketSend is used to send data to a remote computer. SocketSend can be used both for client and server applications.
socketsend.syntax:
SocketSend
[ Socket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev >
[ \Str ':=' < expression (IN) of string > ]
| [ \RawData ':=' < variable (VAR) of rawdata > ]
| [ \Data ':=' < array {*} (IN) of byte > ]
[ '\' NoOfBytes ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

socketsendto.name: SocketSendTo
socketsendto.usage: SocketSendTo is used to send data to a remote computer. SocketSendTo can be used both for client and server applications. SocketSendTo is used for connectionless communication with datagram protocol UDP/IP.
socketsendto.syntax:
SocketSendTo
[ Socket ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of socketdev >
[ RemoteAddress ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string >
[ RemotePort ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num >
[ \Str ':=' < expression (IN) of string > ]
| [ \RawData ':=' < variable (VAR) of rawdata > ]
| [ \Data ':=' < array {*} (IN) of byte > ]
[ '\' NoOfBytes ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

softact.name: SoftAct
softact.usage: SoftAct (Soft Servo Activate) is used to activate the so called "soft" servo on any axis of the robot or external mechanical unit.
softact.syntax:
SoftAct
['\'MechUnit ':=' < variable (VAR) of mecunit>',']
[Axis ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ','
[Softness':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ','
[ '\'Ramp':=' < expression (IN) of num> ]';'

softdeact.name: SoftDeact
softdeact.usage: SoftDeact (Soft Servo Deactivate) is used to deactivate the so called "soft" servo.

speedlimaxis.name: SpeedLimAxis
speedlimaxis.brief: Set speed limitation for an axis
speedlimaxis.usage: SpeedLimAxis is used to set a speed limit value for an axis. The speed reduction is done when the system input signal LimitSpeed is set to 1. With this instruction
speedlimaxis.syntax:
SpeedLimAxis
[ MechUnit':=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[ AxisNo':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ','
[ AxisSpeed':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ';'

speedlimcheckpoint.name: SpeedLimCheckPoint
speedlimcheckpoint.brief: Set speed limitation for check points
speedlimcheckpoint.usage: SpeedLimCheckPoint is used to set a speed limit value for a TCP robot. The speed reduction is done when the system input signal LimitSpeed is set to 1.

speedrefresh.name: SpeedRefresh
speedrefresh.brief: Update speed override for ongoing movement
speedrefresh.usage: SpeedRefresh is used to change the movement speed for the ongoing robot movement in current motion program task. With this instruction it is possible to create some type of coarse speed adaptation from some sensor input. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove system, in any Motion tasks.

spystart.name: SpyStart
spystart.brief: Start recording of execution time data
spystart.usage: SpyStartis used to start the recording of instruction and time data during execution. The execution data will be stored in a file for later analysis. The stored data is intended for debugging RAPID programs, specifically for multi-tasking systems (only necessary to have SpyStart - SpyStop in one program task).

spystop.name: SpyStop
spystop.brief: Stop recording of time execution data
spystop.usage: SpyStopis used to stop the recording of time data during execution. The data, which can be useful for optimizing the execution cycle time, is stored in a file for later analysis.

startload.name: StartLoad
startload.brief: Load a program module during execution
startload.usage: StartLoad is used to start the loading of a program module into the program memory during execution. Static mode Dynamic mode When loading is in progress other instructions can be executed in parallel. The loaded module must be connected to the program task with the instruction WaitLoad before any of its symbols/routines can be used. The loaded program module will be added to the modules already existing in the program memory.
startload.syntax:
StartLoad
['\'Dynamic ',']
[FilePath' :='] <expression (IN) of string>
['\'File ':=' <expression (IN) of string> ] ','
[LoadNo ':='] <variable (VAR) of loadsession>';'

startmove.name: StartMove
startmove.usage: StartMove is used to resume robot, external axes movement and belonging process after the movement has been stopped � by the instruction StopMove. � after execution of StorePath ... RestoPath sequence. � after asynchronously raised movements errors, such as ERR_PATH_STOP or specific process error after handling in the ERROR handler. For base system it is possible to use this instruction in the following type of program tasks:

startmoveretry.name: StartMoveRetry
startmoveretry.usage: StartMoveRetry is used to resume robot and external axes movements and belonging processes and also retry the execution from an ERROR handler.

stcalib.name: STCalib
stcalib.usage: STCalib is used to calibrate the distance between the tool tips. This is necessary after tip change or tool change, and it is recommended after performing a tip dress
stcalib.syntax:
STCalib
[ 'ToolName' :=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ','
[ '\'ToolChg] | ['\'TipChg] | [ '\'TipWear]
[' \'RetTipWear' :=' < variable or persistent(INOUT) of num >
]';'
[ '\'RetPosAdj' :=' < variable or persistent(INOUT) of num > ]';'
[ '\'PrePos' :=' < expression (IN) of num > ]'
[ '\'Conc' ];'

stclose.name: STClose
stclose.usage: STClose is used to close the Servo Tool.
stclose.syntax:
STClose
[ 'ToolName ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ','
[ 'Tipforce' :=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ 'Thickness' :='] < expression (IN) of num > ]
['\' 'RetThickness' :=' < variable or persistent (INOUT) of num
>]
['\'Conc]

stepbwdpath.name: StepBwdPath
stepbwdpath.brief: Move backwards one step on path
stepbwdpath.usage: StepBwdPath is used to move the TCP backwards on the robot path from a RESTART event routine. It is up to the user to introduce a restart process flag so StepBwdPath in the RESTART event routine is only executed at process restart and not at all program restarts. This instruction can only be used in the main task T_ROB1 or, if in a MultiMove System, in Motion tasks.
stepbwdpath.syntax:
StepBwdPath
[ StepLength':=' ] < expression (IN) of num >','
[ StepTime ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num >';'

stindgun.name: STIndGun
stindgun.brief: Sets the gun in independent mode
stindgun.usage: STIndGun(Servo Tool independent gun) is used to set the gun in independent mode and thereafter move the gun to a specified independent position. The gun
stindgun.syntax:
STIndGun ToolName GunPos
Data type: string
The name of the mechanical unit.
Data type: num
The position (stroke) of the servo gun in mm.
STIndGun
[ ToolName ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ','
[ GunPos ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ]';'

stindgunreset.name: STIndGunReset
stindgunreset.brief: Resets the gun from independent mode
stindgunreset.usage: STIndGunReset (Servo Tool independent gun reset) is used to reset the gun from independent mode and thereafter move the gun to current robtarget position.

stoolrotcalib.name: SToolRotCalib
stoolrotcalib.brief: Calibration of TCP and rotation for stationary tool
stoolrotcalib.usage: SToolRotCalib (Stationary Tool Rotation Calibration) is used to calibrate the Description TCP and rotation of a stationary tool. The position of the robot and its movements are always related to its tool coordinate system, i.e. the TCP and tool orientation. To get the best accuracy it is important to define the tool coordinate system as correctly as possible. The calibration can also be done with a manual method using the FlexPendant (described in Operating manual - IRC5 with FlexPendant, section Programming and testing).
stoolrotcalib.syntax:
SToolRotCalib
[ RefTip ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget > ','
[ ZPos ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget > ','
[ XPos ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget > ','
[ Tool ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of tooldata > ';'

stooltcpcalib.name: SToolTCPCalib
stooltcpcalib.brief: Calibration of TCP for stationary tool
stooltcpcalib.usage: SToolTCPCalib (Stationary Tool TCP Calibration) is used to calibrate the Tool Description Center Point - TCP for a stationary tool. The position of the robot and its movements are always related to its tool coordinate system, i.e. the TCP and tool orientation. To get the best accuracy it is important to define the tool coordinate system as correctly as possible. The calibration can also be done with a manual method using the FlexPendant (described in Operating manual - IRC5 with FlexPendant, section Programming and testing).
stooltcpcalib.syntax:
SToolTCPCalib
[ Pos1 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget > ','
[ Pos2 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget > ','
[ Pos3 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget > ','
[ Pos4 ':=' ] < expression (IN) of robtarget > ','
[ Tool ':=' ] < persistent (PERS) of tooldata > ','
[ MaxErr ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of num > ','
[ MeanErr ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of num > ';'
examples: SToolTCPCalib p1, p2, p3, p4, tool1, max_err, mean_err;

stop.name: Stop
stop.usage: Stop is used to stop the program execution. Any movement performed at the time will be finished before the Stop instruction is ready.

stopen.name: STOpen
stopen.usage: STOpen is used to open the Servo Tool.

stopmove.name: StopMove
stopmove.usage: StopMove is used to stop robot and external axes movements and any belonging process temporarily. If the instruction StartMove is given then the movement and process resumes. This instruction can, for example, be used in a trap routine to stop the robot temporarily when an interrupt occurs. For base system it is possible to use this instruction in the following type of program tasks: � main task T_ROB1 for stopping the movement in that task.

stopmovereset.name: StopMoveReset
stopmovereset.brief: Reset the system stop move state
stopmovereset.usage: StopMoveReset is used to reset the system stop move state without starting any movements. Asynchronously raised movements errors, such as ERR_PATH_STOP or specific process error during the movements, can be handled in the ERROR handler. When such an error occurs the movements are stopped at once, and the system stop move flag is set for actual program tasks. This means that the movement is not restarted if doing any program start while program pointer is inside the ERROR handler.

storepath.name: StorePath
storepath.brief: Stores the path when an interrupt occurs
storepath.usage: StorePath is used to store the movement path being executed, e.g. when an error or interrupt occurs. The error handler or a trap routine can then start a new

sttune.name: STTune
sttune.usage: STTune is used to tune/change a servo tool parameter. The parameter is changed temporarily from the original value, which is set up in the system parameters. The new tune value will be active immediately after executing the instruction. STTune is useful in tuning procedures. A tuning procedure is typically used to find an optimal value for a parameter. An experiment (i.e. a program execution with a servo tool movement) is repeated when using different parameter tune values. STTune shall not be used during calibration or tool closure.
sttune.syntax:
STTune
[ MecUnit ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit > ','
[ TuneValue' :=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ 'Type ':='] < expression (IN) of tunegtype > ]';'

sttunereset.name: STTuneReset
sttunereset.usage: STTuneReset is used to restore original values of servo tool parameters if they have been changed by the STTune instruction.

supsyncsensoroff.name: SupSyncSensorOff
supsyncsensoroff.usage: SupSyncSensorOff is used to stop supervision of the robot movement and synchronized sensor movement.

supsyncsensoron.name: SupSyncSensorOn
supsyncsensoron.usage: SupSyncSensorOn is used to start the supervision between robot movement and a synchronized sensor movement.
supsyncsensoron.syntax:
SupSyncSensorOn
[ MechUnit ':=' ] <variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[ MaxSyncSup ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ SafetyDist ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ','
[ MinSyncSup ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num >
[ \SafetyDelay ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ';'

syncmoveoff.name: SyncMoveOff
syncmoveoff.usage: SyncMoveOff is used to end a sequence of synchronized movements and, in most cases, coordinated movements. First, all involved program tasks will wait to synchronize in a stop point, and then the motion planners for the involved program tasks are set to independent mode. The instruction SyncMoveOff can only be used in a MultiMove system with option Coordinated Robots and only in program tasks defined as Motion Task.
syncmoveoff.syntax:
SyncMoveOff
[ SyncID ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of syncident>
[ '\'TimeOut' :=' < expression (IN) of num> ] ';'

syncmoveon.name: SyncMoveOn
syncmoveon.usage: SyncMoveOn is used to start a sequence of synchronized movements and in most cases, coordinated movements. First, all involved program tasks will wait to synchronize in a stop point and then the motion planner for the involved program tasks is set to synchronized mode. The instruction SyncMoveOn can only be used in a MultiMove system with option Coordinated Robots and only in program tasks defined as Motion Task. WARNING To reach safe synchronization functionality every meeting point (parameter
syncmoveon.syntax:
SyncMoveOn
[ SyncID ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of syncident> ','
[ TaskList ':=' ] < persistent array {*} (PERS) of tasks> ','
[ '\' TimeOut ':=' < expression (IN) of num > ] ';'

syncmoveresume.name: SyncMoveResume
syncmoveresume.usage: SyncMoveResume is used to go back to synchronized movements from independent movement mode. The instruction can only be used on StorePath level, e.g. after a StorePath \KeepSync has been executed and the system is in independent motion mode after SyncMoveSuspend has been executed. To be able to use the instruction the system must have been in synchronized motion mode before executing the StorePath and SyncMoveSuspend instruction. The instruction SyncMoveResume can only be used in a MultiMove system with options Coordinated Robots and Path Recovery and only in program tasks defined

syncmovesuspend.name: SyncMoveSuspend
syncmovesuspend.usage: SyncMoveSuspend is used to suspend synchronized movements mode and set the system to independent-semicoordinated movement mode. The instruction can only be used on StorePath level, e.g. after a StorePath or StorePath \KeepSync has been executed and the system is in synchronized movement mode. The instruction SyncMoveSuspend can only be used in a MultiMove System with options Coordinated Robots and Path Recovery and only in program tasks defined as Motion Task.

syncmoveundo.name: SyncMoveUndo
syncmoveundo.usage: SyncMoveUndo is used to force a reset of synchronized coordinated movements and set the system to independent movement mode. The instruction SyncMoveUndo can only be used in a MultiMove system with option Coordinated Robots and only in program tasks defined as Motion Task.

synctosensor.name: SyncToSensor
synctosensor.usage: SyncToSensor is used to start or stop synchronization of robot movement to sensor movement.
synctosensor.syntax:
SyncToSensor
[ MechUnit ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit >
[ \MaxSync] [ '\' On] | [ '\' Off] ';'

systemstopaction.name: SystemStopAction
systemstopaction.usage: SystemStopAction can be used to stop the robot system in different ways depending how serious the error or problem is.

test.name: TEST
test.brief: Depending on the value of an expression
test.usage: TEST is used when different instructions are to be executed depending on the value of an expression or data. If there are not too many alternatives then the IF..ELSE instruction can also be used.
test.syntax:
TEST <expression>
{ CASE <test value> { ',' <test value> } ':'
<statement list> }
[ DEFAULT ':'
<statement list> ]
ENDTEST

testsigndefine.name: TestSignDefine
testsigndefine.usage: TestSignDefine is used to define one test signal for the robot motion system. A test signal continuously mirrors some specified motion data stream. For example,
testsigndefine.syntax:
TestSignDefine
[ Channel ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num>' ,'
[ SignalId' :=' ] < expression (IN) of testsignal> ','
[ MechUnit' :=' ] < variable (VAR) of mecunit> ','
[ Axis ':=' ] < expression (IN) of num> ','
[ SampleTime' :=' ] < expression (IN) of num > ';'

testsignreset.name: TestSignReset
testsignreset.usage: TestSignReset is used to deactivate all previously defined test signals.

texttabinstall.name: TextTabInstall
texttabinstall.usage: TextTabInstall is used to install a text table in the system.

tperase.name: TPErase
tperase.brief: Erases text printed on the FlexPendant
tperase.usage: TPErase (FlexPendant Erase) is used to clear the display of the FlexPendant.

tpreaddnum.name: TPReadDnum
tpreaddnum.brief: Reads a number from the FlexPendant
tpreaddnum.usage: TPReadDnum (FlexPendant Read Numerical) is used to read a number from the
tpreaddnum.syntax:
TPReadDnum
[TPAnswer':='] <var or pers (INOUT) of dnum>','
[TPText':='] <expression (IN) of string>
['\'MaxTime':=' <expression (IN) of num>]
['\'DIBreak':=' <variable (VAR) of signaldi>]
['\'DIPassive]
['\'DOBreak':=' <variable (VAR) of signaldo>]
['\'DOPassive]
['\'PersBoolBreak ':=' <persistent (PERS) of bool>]
['\'PersBoolPassive]
['\'BreakFlag':=' <var or pers (INOUT) of errnum>] ';'
examples: TPReadDnum value, "How many units should be produced?";

tpreadfk.name: TPReadFK
tpreadfk.usage: TPReadFK (FlexPendant Read Function Key) is used to write text on the functions
tpreadfk.syntax:
TPReadFK
[TPAnswer ':='] <var or pers (INOUT) of num>','
[TPText ':='] <expression (IN) of string>','
[TPFK1 ':='] <expression (IN) of string>','
[TPFK2 ':='] <expression (IN) of string>','
[TPFK3 ':='] <expression (IN) of string>','
[TPFK4 ':='] <expression (IN) of string>','
[TPFK5 ':='] <expression (IN) of string>
['\' MaxTime ':=' <expression (IN) of num>]
['\' DIBreak ':=' <variable (VAR) of signaldi>]
['\' DIPassive]
['\' DOBreak ':=' <variable (VAR) of signaldo>]
['\' DOPassive]
['\' Pers

tpreadnum.name: TPReadNum
tpreadnum.brief: Reads a number from the FlexPendant
tpreadnum.usage: TPReadNum (FlexPendant Read Numerical) is used to read a number from the FlexPendant.
tpreadnum.syntax:
TPReadNum
[TPAnswer':='] <var or pers (INOUT) of num>','
[TPText':='] <expression (IN) of string>
['\'MaxTime':=' <expression (IN) of num>]
['\'DIBreak':=' <variable (VAR) of signaldi>]
['\'DIPassive]
['\'DOBreak':=' <variable (VAR) of signaldo>]
['\'DOPassive]
['\'PersBoolBreak ':=' <persistent (PERS) of bool>]
['\'PersBoolPassive]
['\'BreakFlag':=' <var or pers (INOUT) of errnum>] ';'
examples: TPReadNum reg1, "How many units should be produced?";

tpshow.name: TPShow
tpshow.usage: TPShow (FlexPendant Show) is used to select FlexPendant window from RAPID.

tpwrite.name: TPWrite
tpwrite.usage: TPWrite (FlexPendant Write) is used to write text on the FlexPendant. The value of certain data can be written as well as text.
tpwrite.syntax:
TPWrite
[TPText':='] <expression (IN) of string>
['\'Num':=' <expression (IN) of num> ]
| ['\'Bool':=' <expression (IN) of bool> ]
| ['\'Pos':=' <expression (IN) of pos> ]
| ['\'Orient':=' <expression (IN) of orient> ]
| ['\'Dnum':=' <expression (IN) of dnum> ]';'
examples: TPWrite "This task does not control any TCP robot";

triggc.name: TriggC
triggc.usage: TriggC (Trigg Circular) is used to set output signals and/or run interrupt routines at fixed positions at the same time that the robot is moving on a circular path.
examples: TriggC p2, p3, v500, gunon, fine, gun1;
TriggC p1, p2, v500, trigg1, fine, gun1;
TriggC p3, p4, v500, trigg1, fine, gun1;

triggcheckio.name: TriggCheckIO
triggcheckio.brief: Defines I/O check at a fixed position
triggcheckio.usage: TriggCheckIO is used to define conditions for testing the value of a digital, a group of digital, or an analog input or output signal at a fixed position along the
triggcheckio.syntax:
TriggCheckIO
[ TriggData ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of triggdata> ','
[ Distance' :=' ] < expression (IN) of num>
[ '\' Start ] | [ '\' Next ] | [ '\' Time ] ','
[ Signal ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of anytype> ','

writecfgdata.name: WriteCfgData

[ Attribute ':=' ] < expression (IN) of string > ','
[ CfgData ':=' ] < expression (IN) of anytype >
[ '\' ListNo ':=' < variable (VAR) of num >] ';'

writerawbytes.syntax:

[ RawData ':=' ] < variable (VAR) of rawbytes>
[ '\' NoOfBytes ':=' < expression (IN) of num>] ';'

writestrbin.name: WriteStrBin
writestrbin.brief: Writes a string to a binary serial channel
writestrbin.usage: WriteStrBin (Write String Binary) is used to write a string to a binary serial channel or binary file.
writestrbin.syntax:
WriteStrBin
[ IODevice ':='] <variable (VAR) of iodev> ','

writevar.name: WriteVar

writevararr.name: WriteVarArr

wzdisable.syntax:

';'

wzdoset.name: WZDOSet

WZHomeJointDef
[ ['\' Inside] | ['\' Outside] ',']
[ Shape ':='] <variable (VAR) of shapedata> ','

wzlimjointdef.name: WZLimJointDef
wzlimjointdef.brief: Define a world zone for limitation in joints
wzlimjointdef.usage: WZLimJointDef (World Zone Limit Joint Definition) is used to define a world zone in joints coordinates for both the robot and external axes, to be used for limitation of the working area. With WZLimJointDef it is possible to limit the working area for each robot and external axes in the RAPID program, besides the limitation that can be done with system parameters Motion - Arm - robx_y - Upper Joint Bound ... Lower Joint Bound.
wzlimjointdef.syntax:
WZLimJointDef
[ ['\' Inside] | ['\' Outside] ',']
[ Shape ':='] <variable (VAR) of shapedata> ','
[ LowJointVal ':='] <expression (IN) of jointtarget> ','
[ HighJointVal ':='] <expression (IN) of jointtarget> ';'

wzlimsup.name: WZLimSup
wzlimsup.usage: WZLimSup (World Zone Limit Supervision) is used to define the action and to activate a world zone for supervision of the working area of the robot or external
wzlimsup.syntax:
WZLimSup
[ ['\' Temp] | ['\Stat] ',']
[ WorldZone ':='] <variable or persistent (INOUT) of wztemporary>
','
[ Shape ':='] <variable (VAR) of shapedata> ';'

wzsphdef.name: WZSphDef

The input robtarget data should be specified in the same coordinate system as specified in argument for Tool, WObj, and at execution time active program displacement (ProgDisp) and external axes offset (EOffs). The returned jointtarget data is expressed in the calibration coordinate system. If MultiMove application type semicoordinated or synchronized coordinated mode with the coordinated workobject, and is moved by some mechanical unit located
calcjointt.syntax:

The definition of a user frame for a rotational external axis requires that the turntable (or similar mechanical structure) on the external axis has a marked reference point. Moreover, the TCP robot's base frame and TCP must be calibrated. The calibration

[TargetList ':='] <array {*} (IN) of robtarget> ','

[MaxErr ':='] <variable (VAR) of num> ','

A function with a return value of the data type pose.

truncdnum.syntax:

A function with a return value of the data type bool.

valtostr.brief: Converts a value to a string
valtostr.usage: ValToStr (Value To String) is used to convert a value of any data type to a string.
valtostr.syntax:
ValToStr '('
[ Val ':=' ] <expression (IN) of anytype> ')'
A function with a return value of the data type string.
